action.reasoning: Observation: The heuristic planner crashes with IndexError in bfs_path because the graph is built once at episode start but the environment dynamically adds nodes as new free space is discovered. When select_action uses global_best_idx derived from current obs['nodes'] (which may have more nodes than the initial graph), bfs_path uses a seen list of length self.n_nodes (original) and goal_idx may exceed that, causing index out of range. Analysis: Rebuilding the graph each step will fix this and allow correct BFS to newly added nodes. The previous successful iteration (13) likely used a static graph but its environment version might not have dynamic node addition? Regardless, rebuilding each step is safe and correct. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with a UtilityGreedyPlanner that rebuilds graph on every select_action call (or at each step), robust fallbacks, and no early termination. Test locally to verify success > 0, then submit evaluation to regain baseline ~0.55 and potentially exceed it. Remaining iterations: 2, time: 3.15h — sufficient for one more eval submission.
